flash_sale_db_bottleneck.text: Your Main DB is hitting 100% CPU. How will you scale?

choices.impact: A cache reduces read load by 90%. Great for static or slow-changing data.
Replica handles read traffic, but increases consistency complexity.
Simple, but expensive and has a hard ceiling.

global_latency_cdn.text: Users in Asia are seeing 2s latency. What is your first move?

choices.impact: CDNs serve static assets from the edge, closer to users.
Lowers latency but vastly increases data replication complexity.

chat_reliability_bottleneck.text: Your real-time chat service is dropping messages during peak usage. How do you handle the surge?

choices.impact: Decouples producers from consumers. Great for reliability and async processing.
Direct push is fast but requires more connection management on the server.

newsfeed_fanout_conflict.text: Your 'Write-time Fanout' is slowing down for celebrities with 1M followers. What's the better feed strategy?

choices.impact: Fan out for normal users, but pull for 'mega-users' at read-time. Saves write-amplification.
Distribute feed writes across more shards. Faster reads but more complex writes.

distributed_lock_contention.text: Multiple servers are trying to update the same user balance simultaneously, causing race conditions. How will you coordinate?

choices.impact: Simple, high-performance locking. But beware of clock-skew issues.
Strongly consistent and reliable, but slower and more operationally complex.

serverless_cold_start_crisis.text: Your Lambda-based checkout service is timing out during peak load. Mitigate the cold start.

choices.impact: Eliminates cold starts by keeping functions warm, but increases costs.
Moving to containers avoids cold start overhead for steady-state traffic.

egress_cost_explosion.text: Your inter-region data transfer is costing more than your compute. How will you optimize?

choices.impact: Keep reads within the same region to avoid egress fees.
Optimizes network paths across the global backbone, potentially reducing egress.

pii_data_leak_prevention.text: An auditor found PII in your debug logs. What is your immediate fix?

choices.impact: Automatically redacts PII before it reaches the logging service.
Replace sensitive data with non-sensitive tokens throughout the system.

rag_latency_surge.text: Your RAG pipeline p99 latency has spiked to 5s. Users are abandoning queries. How do you fix it?

choices.impact: HNSW index tuning and quantization reduce retrieval from 800ms to 50ms, but recall may drop 2-3%.
Cache semantically similar queries. 60-70% hit rate for repeated question patterns, dramatically cuts LLM calls.

llm_cost_explosion.text: Your GPT-4 bill hit 10x budget this month. Product wants to keep quality high. How do you cut costs?

choices.impact: Route simple queries to smaller models, escalate complex ones to GPT-4. Cuts cost 70% with <5% quality loss.
Cache frequent responses and compress prompts by 40%. Reduces tokens but may miss nuance in edge cases.

cqrs_rebuild_storm.text: Your CQRS read model is 2 hours behind writes after a deployment. Users see stale data everywhere. How do you catch up?

choices.impact: Spin up parallel consumers to rebuild projections concurrently. Fast, but requires idempotent handlers.
Restore from last snapshot, then replay only recent events. Faster recovery but requires snapshot infrastructure.

hot_partition_stampede.text: One DynamoDB partition is at 95% while others sit idle. A viral product is causing a hot key. What's your move?

choices.impact: Add virtual nodes and re-hash the key space. Distributes load evenly but requires a migration window.
Buffer writes in cache, flush to DB in batches. Absorbs spikes but risks data loss on cache failure.

microservice_death_spiral.text: Cascading timeouts across 12 services — one slow upstream is taking everything down. How do you stop the bleeding?

choices.impact: Fail fast when upstream is unhealthy. Prevents cascade but returns errors to some users during recovery.
Isolate each service's thread pool. Slow service can't starve others, but reduces overall throughput ceiling.

dns_failover_cascade.text: Primary region is down. DNS TTL is 300s and users are still hitting the dead endpoint. How do you fail over?

choices.impact: Both regions serve traffic simultaneously. Zero failover time but doubles infrastructure cost and complexity.
Standby region with Route53 health checks. 60-90s failover, lower cost, but brief downtime window.

event_sourcing_replay.text: Your event store replay is taking 6 hours for a full rebuild. A bug corrupted the last projection. How do you recover?

choices.impact: Snapshot aggregate state hourly. Replay only from last snapshot — 6hrs becomes 45min. Adds storage overhead.
Partition event stream and replay in parallel across workers. Linear speedup but requires partition-safe projections.

rate_limiter_thundering_herd.text: Your rate limiter resets every minute, causing a thundering herd of requests at the boundary. How do you smooth it?

choices.impact: Weighted average of current and previous window. Smooth traffic but slightly more complex to implement.
Tokens refill continuously with random jitter. Eliminates boundary spikes and allows controlled bursts.

cross_region_replication_lag.text: 800ms replication lag between US-East and EU-West. Users editing the same document see stale versions. What's the fix?

choices.impact: Route reads to the region where the write occurred. Guarantees freshness for the writer, not other readers.
Data structures that merge automatically without coordination. Eventually consistent but zero conflicts.

ml_model_serving_cold_start.text: Your ML model takes 30s to load on new instances. Autoscaling events leave users waiting. How do you fix cold starts?

choices.impact: Pre-bake models into container images. Instances start with model ready — 30s becomes 3s. Larger images though.
Knowledge-distill to a 10x smaller model. 2s load time, 85% of accuracy. May underperform on edge cases.
